speaker: Protest Square NPCs / Affected Persons
description: Dedicated voice-reference dialogue samples for Chapter 5 Protest Square NPCs and affected persons in Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Dedicated Chapter 5 Protest Square and affected-person crowd file
Expands beyond generic street NPCs with evidence-bearing affected persons

Provides impact-statement donors, route helpers, protest marshals, notice recipients, portal-failure witnesses, legal-service users, clinic users, small businesses, community organisers, and vulnerable persons
Supports Chapter 5 final proof trail: notice clarity, individual assessment, objection filter, contractor conflict, social utility, and remedy
Includes pre-Chapter foreshadow/callback lines so some affected persons can appear earlier and feel like part of the world
Uses variant_id, affected_status, and evidence_use fields for mechanical filtering

voice_profile.core_voice: grounded, human, varied, concrete, short enough for RPG dialogue, with legal stakes translated into everyday consequences
comedy_engine: people react to high administrative language with practical confusion
protest organisation becomes absurdly procedural because the city made everyone learn forms
ordinary people are funny because they are clearer than officials
the crowd understands the case through doors, buses, queues, phones, bills, and notices
the protest is lively without flattening vulnerable people into jokes
do: make affected people concrete rather than abstract
show why the Legal Quarter matters as an access ecosystem
make impact statements usable as evidence
distinguish anger, confusion, exhaustion, humour, and practical solidarity
let some NPCs support reform in principle while objecting to the process
keep lines mechanically searchable by issue tags
do_not: make affected persons generic victims only
make fun of disability, poverty, age, language difficulty, or vulnerability
turn every line into a slogan
make the city purely evil through crowd lines

variant_ids.protest_marshal: Crowd organisers and route helpers who direct the player through Protest Square.
variant_ids.notice_recipient: People directly affected by unclear mass notices.
variant_ids.portal_failure_witness: People whose objections failed, vanished, or were misclassified.
variant_ids.legal_aid_client: People who rely on Pig & Swine or similar low-threshold legal help.
variant_ids.clinic_user: Patients and clinic/community-service users affected by relocation.
variant_ids.small_business_owner: Local shops/services affected by redevelopment and notice defects.
variant_ids.tenant_resident: Residents facing housing uncertainty under the redevelopment scheme.
variant_ids.elderly_resident: Older residents whose access barriers make the notice/portal process concrete.
variant_ids.disabled_person: Disabled or mobility-impaired affected persons focused on practical access.
variant_ids.parent_carer: Parents and carers dealing with deadlines, childcare, and access barriers.
variant_ids.language_barrier_person: People needing plain-language or translation support.
variant_ids.community_volunteer: Volunteers collecting forms, statements, screenshots, and supplies.
variant_ids.moderate_reformer: Affected people who accept reform in principle but criticise process.
variant_ids.angry_protester: Louder protest voices, useful for crowd energy but less legally precise.
variant_ids.quiet_witness: Low-key witnesses whose specific details are strong evidence.
variant_ids.press_adjacent_citizen: Affected people speaking near cameras without becoming reporter NPCs.
variant_ids.postgame_affected_person: Post-judgment affected persons reacting to remedies and next steps.

evidence_uses.notice_clarity: Supports argument that notices were not practically understandable.
evidence_uses.individual_assessment: Supports argument that category-level handling missed concrete circumstances.
evidence_uses.objection_filter: Supports argument that objections were misclassified, lost, or not meaningfully reviewed.
evidence_uses.contractor_safeguards: Supports argument that technical implementation and throughput incentives mattered.
evidence_uses.social_utility: Supports argument that Pig & Swine / Legal Quarter served a real access function.
evidence_uses.remedy: Supports controlled remedies: corrected notices, restored objections, reassessment, interim protection.
evidence_uses.flavor: Ambient worldbuilding, jokes, protest texture, or route guidance.

notes: Every line is a voice-reference draft for later rewriting into godot/data/dialogues/dialogues.json. Use variant_id + affected_status + evidence_use to filter mechanically.
